Container.Name: Lab Drop
Container.Overview: Lab Drop is a self-hosted homepage for your homelab/server, with a free-2D drag-and-drop tile layout and a hardened backend (fail-closed secrets, deny-by-default auth on state-changing endpoints, command-injection and path-traversal fixes). Runs as a non-root user (PUID/PGID). A fork of lab-dash by Anthony Gress, licensed under GPL-3.0 — all credit for the original project goes to the upstream author and contributors.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: dashboard homepage homelab server labdrop lab-drop labdash lab-dash
Container.Requires: Optional: for network-usage monitoring stats, set Privileged and use host networking.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Stores users.json, config.json, and the auto-generated .secret

Config.Name: Uploads Path
Config.Description: Stores uploaded icons and background images

Config.Name: Secret Key
Config.Description: Optional. Leave blank to auto-generate a per-install key (persisted to /config/.secret on first run). Only set it to manage the key yourself, e.g. `openssl rand -base64 32`. Do not reuse a placeholder value.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID to run as (Unraid default: 99 = nobody). App files in appdata are owned by this UID.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID to run as (Unraid default: 100 = users).

Config.Name: System Path (sensors)
Config.Description: Optional, read-only. Enables temperature/sensor readings. Remove if unused.
